business_connection: Premium perception — tight headline tracking is now synonymous with Apple quality
takeaway: Marketing type can be expressive; product type stays neutral

font: Google Sans (headings), Roboto (UI) — both humanist sans

headline_style.role_system: Display / Headline / Title / Body / Label each with Large/Medium/Small

body_style.note: Strict role enforcement — no arbitrary sizes

business_connection: Scales across Android, web, and TV without inconsistency
takeaway: Naming roles (not sizes) forces intentional usage

body_style.opacity_hierarchy: opacity-90 primary / opacity-65 secondary / opacity-40 tertiary

business_connection: Professional tool perception — developers pay premium for dense, precise UIs
takeaway: Opacity hierarchy adapts automatically to dark mode — no separate dark tokens needed for text

business_connection: The gap between expressive marketing and calm product type signals 'trustworthy financial tool'
takeaway: The tone of your product type says as much as the content

font: Geist Sans + Geist Mono (both open-source, available on Google Fonts)
business_connection: Became the default in Next.js 14+ — type became infrastructure
takeaway: Investing in type system pays compound interest — every project using Next.js promotes it

font: Anthropic Sans (proprietary), system mono fallback for code
business_connection: AI product that needs to feel intelligent but not intimidating
takeaway: Restraint in weight (never bold) communicates confidence without aggression
